diversity_check: PASSED — three distinct bridge mechanisms: nuclear architecture spatial filter (H4-v2), sequential enzymatic gate (H2-v2), YAP-independent MRTF/SRF CArG program (H5-v2)

hypotheses.title: Constitutive LAD Enhancers as Hard-Wired Stiffness Resistance Nodes — Lamin A/C Tethering Creates a Binary Mechanical Gate Encoded in Nuclear Geometry
hypotheses.evolution_summary: Replaces '>90% non-LAD' with odds ratio > 4 (properly null-controlled). Replaces siLMNA (YAP confound) with LMNA-R386K point mutant (preserves nuclear shape, weakens chromatin tethering). Adds cLAD vs fLAD stratification as primary predictive variable. Adds stiffness dose-dependence of OR as secondary prediction.
hypotheses.novelty_claim: No study has tested whether constitutive LAD-embedded enhancers with TEAD motifs are categorically stiffness-resistant while non-LAD TEAD enhancers are stiffness-responsive, nor quantified this as an enrichment odds ratio separable from random LAD genome coverage. The cLAD/fLAD distinction and LMNA-R386K rescue are novel experimental elements.
hypotheses.mechanism_route: Stiff ECM -> lamin A/C upregulation (Swift 2013) -> reinforced cLAD anchoring -> HDAC2 proximity maintained (LMNA-HDAC2 STRING 0.690) -> H3K9me3 preserved at cLAD-embedded enhancers -> YAP/EP300 cannot deposit H3K27ac at cLAD TEAD sites -> stiffness-activated H3K27ac confined to non-LAD TEAD enhancers. Binary filter emergent from nuclear geometry.
hypotheses.key_quantitative_prediction: Odds ratio > 4 for H3K27ac gain (>2-fold) at non-LAD TEAD enhancers vs cLAD TEAD enhancers under stiff ECM. Odds ratio dose-dependent with stiffness.
hypotheses.key_perturbation: LMNA-R386K point mutant: maintains nuclear stiffness/shape, weakens chromatin-lamina tethering. If cLAD TEAD enhancers partially gain H3K27ac on stiff ECM in R386K cells, LAD tethering (not nuclear shape) is the gate.
critical_revisions_incorporated: Null expectation for >90% prediction replaced with OR > 4 (properly powered)
siLMNA confound addressed: replaced with LMNA-R386K point mutant
cLAD vs fLAD stratification added — prediction strongest for cLADs only
hypotheses.primary_falsification: If OR < 2 for non-LAD vs cLAD TEAD enhancer stiffness-responsiveness, the LAD filter is not operating. If LMNA-R386K does not rescue H3K27ac at cLAD TEAD enhancers on stiff ECM, LAD tethering is not the gate.

hypotheses.title: Sequentially Gated Bivalent Enhancer Activation — KDM6B Demethylation as Rate-Limiting Mechanical Gate Precedes EP300 Acetylation at Developmental Loci in MSCs
hypotheses.evolution_summary: Converts the 'simultaneous coordination' flaw into a testable sequential gate model with a lag-phase kinetic signature (4-8 hr for demethylation, 8-16 hr for acetylation burst). Adds siKDM6B/siKDM6A genetic dissection. Replaces A-485 alone with A-485 + SGC-CBP30 for full HAT blockade. Adds Yu 2025 thyroid cancer caveat. Removes the 'stripped enhancer' prediction (vulnerable to rapid PRC2 re-methylation) and replaces with kinetically resolvable time-course signatures.
hypotheses.novelty_claim: No paper has tested KDM6B (or UTX) as a rate-limiting temporal gate for EP300-dependent H3K27ac deposition at bivalent developmental enhancers under ECM stiffness, nor demonstrated the lag-phase kinetic signature (demethylation precedes acetylation by 4-8 hr) predicted by the sequential gate model.
hypotheses.mechanism_route: Stiff ECM -> KDM6B transcriptional induction (4-8 hr lag; via NF-kB or EGR1) -> H3K27me3 demethylation at bivalent H3K4me1+ enhancers -> exposes unmodified H3K27 -> pre-positioned EP300 (recruited by YAP at 15-60 min but stalled by PRC2-compacted chromatin) catalyzes burst H3K27ac deposition (8-16 hr) -> bivalent-to-active enhancer conversion at developmental loci (RUNX2, SOX9, PPARG).
hypotheses.key_quantitative_prediction: H3K27ac gain at bivalent enhancers lags H3K27me3 loss by 4-8 hr (detectable by cross-correlation of time-course CUT&Tag signals). A 2-hr stiff ECM pulse does not activate bivalent enhancers; an 8-hr pulse does.
hypotheses.key_perturbation: siKDM6B + siKDM6A separately (not just GSK-J4). A-485 (3 uM) + SGC-CBP30 (10 uM) combination for complete HAT blockade. Time-course CUT&Tag at t=0, 2, 4, 8, 16, 24, 72 hr post-transfer to stiff ECM.
critical_revisions_incorporated: siKDM6B and siKDM6A separately (to establish which demethylase at enhancers)
Temporal asynchrony reframed as sequential gate with testable lag-phase signature
Yu 2025 thyroid cancer extrapolation to MSCs explicitly acknowledged
A-485 + SGC-CBP30 combination for full HAT blockade (CBP compensation addressed)
hypotheses.primary_falsification: If H3K27ac at bivalent enhancers rises before 4 hr (before KDM6B can accumulate), the gate model is wrong. If siKDM6B does not preserve H3K27me3 but siKDM6A fully phenocopies GSK-J4, UTX (not KDM6B) is the demethylase.

hypotheses.title: MRTF/SRF-Dependent CArG Enhancer Remodeling Under ECM Stiffness — A YAP-Independent Mechanosensitive Enhancer Program with Distinct Nuclear Localization Preferences
hypotheses.operation: mutation (mechanosensor swap: YAP/BRD4 → MRTF/SRF) + crossover with H4 (nuclear architecture insight applied to CArG enhancers)
hypotheses.evolution_summary: Parent H5 abandoned due to quantitative fatal flaw (H3K27ac half-life 2-6 hr vs claimed 24-72 hr memory). Mutation swaps the mechanosensor from YAP to MRTF-A (actin-polymerization-dependent, YAP-independent). Crossover with H4 applies the nuclear architecture framing to ask whether CArG enhancers show similar or different LAD distribution to TEAD enhancers. The result is a hypothesis with a fully distinct upstream pathway (RhoA-F-actin-MRTF, not integrin-FAK-LATS-YAP), a distinct TF (SRF, not TEAD), a distinct enhancer set (CArG elements, not TEAD motifs), and a distinct temporal prediction (MRTF as first-responder preceding YAP by 10-20 min). Directly addresses Critic meta-critique systematic gap #1 (MRTF/SRF enhancer program missing from Cycle 1).
hypotheses.novelty_claim: No paper has profiled MRTF/SRF-dependent enhancer H3K27ac changes under ECM stiffness gradients, compared the CArG-enhancer program to the TEAD-enhancer program at identical stiffness conditions in the same cells, or tested whether MRTF nuclear translocation kinetics make it a 'first-responder' mechanosensitive TF preceding YAP at the enhancer level.
hypotheses.mechanism_route: Stiff ECM -> RhoA/ROCK activation -> F-actin polymerization -> G-actin depletion -> RPEL domain of MRTF-A released from G-actin sequestration -> MRTF-A nuclear translocation (15-30 min; first-responder) -> MRTF-A/SRF at CArG elements -> EP300 or Mediator recruitment -> H3K27ac at distal CArG enhancers controlling cytoskeletal genes (ACTA2, VCL, TAGLN). Parallel to but temporally preceding YAP/TEAD program. Pharmacologically dissectable by cytochalasin D (MRTF) vs verteporfin (YAP).
hypotheses.key_quantitative_prediction: Less than 20% overlap between stiffness-responsive CArG enhancers and stiffness-responsive TEAD enhancers genome-wide. MRTF nuclear translocation precedes YAP by 10-20 min in acute stiffness shift experiments. H3K27ac gain at CArG enhancers correlates linearly with MRTF-A nuclear intensity (not YAP intensity).
hypotheses.key_perturbation: Cytochalasin D (1 uM, 30 min): reduces MRTF nuclear localization without direct YAP effect. Verteporfin (1 uM, 1 hr): YAP/TEAD inhibitor without direct MRTF effect. Comparing H3K27ac at CArG vs TEAD enhancers under each inhibitor decouples the two programs.
critical_revisions_incorporated: Memory half-life flaw resolved by abandoning memory mechanism entirely (mutation operation)
Triptolide control resolved by removing feedback loop mechanism
Critic meta-critique gap #1 (MRTF/SRF) directly addressed by mechanosensor swap
Crossover with H4 (nuclear architecture framing) adds CArG/TEAD LAD comparison as secondary prediction
hypotheses.primary_falsification: If CArG and TEAD stiffness-responsive enhancers overlap >50%, the two programs are not orthogonal. If cytochalasin D also depletes YAP nuclear localization in MCF10A, the pathways cannot be pharmacologically decoupled.

evolution_quality_check.diversity_note: Three distinct bridge mechanisms: nuclear architecture spatial filter (H4-v2), sequential enzymatic gating (H2-v2), YAP-independent MRTF/SRF CArG program (H5-v2). No shared bridge mechanism.

improvement_notes.H4-v2: OR > 4 threshold is properly powered. LMNA-R386K isolates LAD tethering from YAP dynamics. cLAD/fLAD stratification sharpens prediction. Groundedness increased to 8.
improvement_notes.H2-v2: Sequential gate reframing removes the temporal asynchrony flaw and converts it into a positive testable prediction. siKDM6B/siKDM6A dissection and full HAT blockade remove the two main confounds identified by Critic.
improvement_notes.H5-v2: Mechanosensor mutation to MRTF/SRF removes the quantitatively implausible memory mechanism. Addresses the Critic's #1 systematic gap. Crossover with H4 adds integrative value.

evolution_quality_check.critic_mandatory_revisions_met: All mandatory revisions from Critic incorporated across the three evolved hypotheses.

quantitative_plausibility.H4-v2_OR4_threshold: PLAUSIBLE — 65% non-LAD baseline; OR=4 gives ~88% non-LAD expected in responsive set; detectable with Fisher's exact test at n=100-200 enhancers
quantitative_plausibility.H2-v2_lag_4-8hr: PLAUSIBLE — KDM6B protein accumulation after transcriptional induction requires 2-6 hr; consistent with 4-8 hr lag before effective demethylation
quantitative_plausibility.H5-v2_MRTF_temporal_lead: CONDITIONAL — MRTF via G-actin release has fewer steps than YAP via LATS cascade; 10-20 min lead is plausible but may be within experimental noise
